# Full Paper Memory

This is the persistent Codex memory for the Samsung Best Paper Awards full paper.

## Current objective

Create a full paper from the seed two-page draft and project evidence. The paper must be useful to internal manufacturing reviewers and academically defensible.

## Cycle 249 - 2026-06-26 manager guide absolute-rule registration

User request:
- A manager reviewed the paper-summary direction and asked for clearer Introduction/background, semiconductor field-workflow explanation, DRAM cell/block explanation, EDS/FBM/measure-value definitions, bin-map illustration, web-app contribution, HBM-including production-line application/evaluation, workflow architecture, Method/Result separation, limitations/future work, and about 20 related papers.
- User asked to first organize this as absolute rules.

Files updated:
- `D:\project\fbm_paper_full\PAPER_FULL_RULES.md`
- `D:\project\fbm_paper_full\CLAUDE.md`
- `D:\project\fbm_paper_full\docs\agents\full_paper_agents.md`
- `D:\project\fbm_paper_full\docs\memory\full_paper_memory.md`

New absolute-rule content:
- Introduction must assume many reviewers do not know EDS, wafer/bin map, FBM, chip-level measure workflow, or why it matters to quality/yield.
- Introduction must first summarize latest wafer/chip-level failure-analysis research, then explain the real field gap.
- Field workflow must be explained as: chip-level EDS fail/healthy check -> measure-value rule/formula classification -> FBM/TEM physical confirmation -> process structure/layout causal interpretation -> process failure control.
- DRAM cell, cell block, EDS, failbit, Failbit Map, measure value, and bin map must be explained with a figure or text diagram.
- Known/Unknown definitions and engineer-use difficulties must be explicit.
- Workflow architecture must connect measure value / FBM / bin map -> Known classification -> registered-failure filtering -> contrastive embedding/clustering -> user confirmation of new failure registration -> web app functions.
- Contribution list must include web app implementation as the 4th contribution and production-line product application/evaluation including HBM as the 5th contribution, but without unsupported extra numbers or overbroad scope.
- Method section should hold theory/architecture; Result section should use figures/tables/system captures/web-app screenshots/bin map/FBM examples.
- Related work should expand to about 20 primary-source papers; user-provided H. Lee et al. 2024 chip-level wafer bin map paper is a candidate but title/venue/year must be verified before citation.
- Limitations and future work must be separated at the end.
- All additions remain subject to the 4-10 page limit; adding references/figures requires compression or replacement, not simple accumulation.

Terminology carryover:
- Paper-visible text and metadata should avoid `defect`; use `failure`, `fail`, or `불량`.
- Exact reference titles are the exception and must remain accurate.

Next controlled cycle target:
1. Run literature/background agent against primary sources and local references.
2. Inspect `D:\project\mapviewer` for web-app screenshot feasibility.
3. Plan rev117 page budget before drafting because 20 references plus new figures can easily exceed 10 pages.
4. Draft rev117 only after deciding which existing rev116 content/figures must be compressed or replaced.
